# ADR-NNN: <decision title>

> **Status**: proposed | accepted | superseded by ADR-MMM | deprecated
> **Date**: YYYY-MM-DD
> **Deciders**: <you, others if relevant>
> **Related**: PRD `docs/prds/feat-X.md` (if applicable), ADR-MMM (if relevant)

## Context

What is the situation that requires a decision? Include:

- The forces at play — technical, organisational, performance, cost.
- The constraints that bound the decision space.
- Why this decision needs to be made now, rather than deferred.

Keep this short — two or three paragraphs. If you can't state the context concisely, you may be trying to record more than one decision; split the ADR.

## Decision

The decision, stated as a clear declarative sentence followed by elaboration as needed.

> We will <do X> using <approach Y>.

Then a paragraph or two on what this means in practice.

## Alternatives Considered

The options that were on the table and why they were rejected. This section is the most valuable part of the ADR six months from now — it tells future-you which doors are already closed and why, so you don't re-litigate them.

### Alternative A: <name>
What it was. Why it was rejected.

### Alternative B: <name>
What it was. Why it was rejected.

## Consequences

What becomes true after this decision is enacted. Include both intended consequences (the benefits we're buying) and accepted trade-offs (the costs we're paying).

**Positive:**
- <intended outcome>

**Negative / accepted trade-offs:**
- <cost or constraint we now live with>

**Follow-ups:**
- <work this decision creates, e.g. "migration from old approach tracked in #N">
